title: Writing Composition
objective: Students will explore writing composition concepts including Persuasive Writing, Writing Techniques for Effect, Research & Source Evaluation and more.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Persuasive Writing!

Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence — including introducing claims, acknowledging counterclaims, organising reasons logically, maintaining a formal style, and providing a concluding statement
Let me show you something cool. Write an argument essay with a clear claim supported by at least three pieces of evidence
If you wants to convince you to let them stay up later, can they write down clear reasons and examples to support their case rather than just saying "because I want to"?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Persuasive Writing. Write an argument essay with a clear claim supported by at least three pieces of evidence Acknowledge and respond to a counterclaim rather than ignoring opposing views
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Writing Techniques for Effect!

Apply growing knowledge of vocabulary, grammar, and text structure to writing — drawing on literary and rhetorical devices from reading (e.g., rhetorical questions, tricolon, anaphora, contrast) to enhance impact
Let me show you something cool. Use a rhetorical question or tricolon deliberately in a persuasive piece and explain the intended effect
When you edits a piece of their writing, do they make choices about word order, sentence structure, or vocabulary to make the writing more powerful or clearer?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Writing Techniques for Effect: Use a rhetorical question or tricolon deliberately in a persuasive piece and explain the intended effect Employ varied sentence openings and structures to maintain reader interest across a whole piece
Let's discover the amazing world of Research & Source Evaluation!

Summarise and organise material from reading and research — gathering relevant information from multiple sources, assessing credibility, integrating evidence without plagiarising, and supporting ideas with factual detail
Let me show you something cool. Take notes from multiple sources on a topic and organise them under logical headings
If you is preparing for a school project, can they gather notes from several sources and organise them into a logical order before they start writing?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Research & Source Evaluation: Take notes from multiple sources on a topic and organise them under logical headings Paraphrase information from a source accurately without copying word-for-word
Let's explore Planning, Revising and Editing Writing! Have you ever wondered about this?

Plan, revise, and edit writing to improve coherence and effectiveness — considering how the writing reflects its intended audience and purpose, amending vocabulary, grammar, and structure, and proofreading for accurate spelling, punctuation, and grammar
Let's practice together! Revise a draft to improve paragraph structure, adding or removing material for coherence
After you finishes a first draft, do they go back and make real improvements — not just fixing spelling, but changing sentences or reorganising ideas to make it better?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Planning, Revising and Editing Writing: Revise a draft to improve paragraph structure, adding or removing material for coherence Edit sentence-level issues including word choice, grammar, and punctuation before finalising
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Writing Across Genres!

Write for a range of purposes and audiences beyond narrative — including scripts, poetry, personal and formal letters, notes for talks, and other forms — selecting the appropriate form, register, and conventions for each
Time to try this out: Write a formal letter using appropriate layout, salutation, register, and sign-off conventions
Can you write in different styles depending on the task — for example, a persuasive letter, a set of instructions, or a diary entry — and make each one sound appropriate?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Writing Across Genres. Write a formal letter using appropriate layout, salutation, register, and sign-off conventions Compose a script with stage directions, character names, and realistic dialogue
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Writing Character & Dialogue!

Write narratives that develop real or imagined experiences using effective technique — including establishing context and point of view, developing characters through dialogue, pacing, and description, using varied transitions, and providing a reflective conclusion
Let me show you something cool. Open a narrative by establishing setting, point of view, and a hook that engages the reader
When you writes a story, do they use techniques like describing feelings, building suspense, or showing dialogue to make it interesting to read?

Great job! Today we learned about Writing Character & Dialogue. Open a narrative by establishing setting, point of view, and a hook that engages the reader Use dialogue, pacing, and descriptive detail to develop characters and advance the plot
Have you ever thought about Developed Informational and Explanatory Writing? Let's find out together!

Write informative and explanatory texts that examine a topic and convey ideas clearly — organising information logically with headings and formatting, developing the topic with relevant facts, definitions, details, and quotations, and using precise vocabulary
Time to try this out: Organise an explanatory essay with a clear introduction, logical body paragraphs, and a conclusion
Can you write a clear explanation of how something works — like how a volcano erupts or how bread is made — so that someone who's never learned about it would understand?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Developed Informational and Explanatory Writing: Organise an explanatory essay with a clear introduction, logical body paragraphs, and a conclusion Develop a topic using well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, and quotations from sources
Today we're going to learn about Cohesion and Transitions Across Writing. Get ready for an adventure!

Use varied transitions, cohesive devices, and paragraph-linking strategies to create coherence across a whole piece of writing — including temporal transitions, causal connectives, and techniques for signalling shifts in argument, time, or setting
Let's practice together! Use a range of causal and logical connectives (consequently, nevertheless, as a result) appropriately across a discursive essay
When you writes several paragraphs, do they use linking phrases like "however," "as a result," or "on the other hand" to connect ideas smoothly between sections?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Cohesion and Transitions Across Writing: Use a range of causal and logical connectives (consequently, nevertheless, as a result) appropriately across a discursive essay Signal a shift in time or setting within a narrative using a transitional phrase or paragraph break
Let's explore Planning Narratives! Have you ever wondered about this?
Plan narrative writing by considering how authors have developed characters and settings, drawing on techniques observed in texts read, heard, or performed
Let me show you something cool. Identify specific authorial techniques for character development from a mentor text
When you is about to write a story, do they think ahead about what their main character is like and where the story will take place before they start writing?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Planning Narratives: Identify specific authorial techniques for character development from a mentor text Plan a character using techniques such as showing through action borrowed from a studied author
Today we're going to learn about Research & Note-Taking. Get ready for an adventure!
Gather relevant information from print and digital sources, summarise or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a basic bibliography or list of sources
Let me show you something cool. Take organised notes from multiple sources using own words rather than copying
If you is researching a topic for a school project, can they find information from books or websites, put it in their own words, and keep track of where they got it from?

Great job! Today we learned about Research & Note-Taking. Take organised notes from multiple sources using own words rather than copying Create a bibliography listing sources used in a research project

lessons.title: Layout and Formatting in Informational Writing

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Layout and Formatting in Informational Writing. Get ready for an adventure!
Use layout devices including headings, sub-headings, columns, bullets, and tables to structure text and guide the reader through informational and explanatory writing
Let me show you something cool. Organise an informational text using headings and sub-headings to separate sections
When you writes a non-fiction piece — like a guide or an explanation — do they use layout features like headings, bullet points, and columns to help the reader navigate the information easily?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Layout and Formatting in Informational Writing. Organise an informational text using headings and sub-headings to separate sections Present comparative information using a table with clear row and column headers
Let's discover the amazing world of Writing a Précis!
Precis longer passages by summarising the main ideas and key information concisely while maintaining the essential meaning and removing non-essential detail
Time to try this out: Reduce a three-paragraph text to a single paragraph capturing all main points
If you reads a long article about a topic they're studying, can they write a short summary in their own words that captures the key points without copying sentences?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Writing a Précis. Reduce a three-paragraph text to a single paragraph capturing all main points Identify and remove redundant information while preserving essential meaning
